novel_features.name: Budget autopilot (ROAS + frequency + utilization)
novel_features.description: Per-campaign INCREASE/DECREASE/HOLD/FLAG recommendations based on ROAS vs target, frequency saturation, and budget utilization. Three strategies (conservative/moderate/aggressive) with per-campaign caps and learning-period awareness.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining live insights with local budget limits, frequency thresholds, and past decisions. No MCP encodes this decisioning logic.

novel_features.name: Batch apply with validation KPIs
novel_features.description: Applies a batch of budget changes from a 'recommend' preview file with a required CONFIRM token, logs each change with validation KPIs and a follow-up date so you can audit outcomes later.
novel_features.rationale: Requires appending structured audit entries with validation_kpis and follow_up_date. No other tool tracks decision outcomes.

novel_features.name: Attribution double-count verification
novel_features.description: Detects when Meta returns both omni_purchase and purchase in the same response, causing dashboards to double-count revenue. Reports the legacy-combined vs deduplicated ROAS delta per campaign.
novel_features.rationale: Requires priority-ordered selection across action types. Domain knowledge encoded from the magoosh-founder production CLI.

novel_features.name: Frequency capacity signal
novel_features.description: Per-campaign has-headroom/confidence derived from frequency, budget utilization, and delivery status. Defaults: saturation at freq>3.5, warning at freq>=2.5.
novel_features.rationale: Meta has no 'lost impression share budget' field like Google. The capacity signal is derived — no MCP ships the heuristic.

novel_features.name: Decision log search + follow-ups due
novel_features.description: FTS5-indexed audit log. 'history search' finds decisions by reasoning text; 'history due' lists applied decisions whose follow-up window has passed and have no analysis yet.
novel_features.rationale: SQLite + FTS5 over append-only decision entries — not possible without a local store.

novel_features.name: Decision post-mortem
novel_features.description: Attach an outcome analysis to a past decision log entry (success/partial/failure/inconclusive) with observation and hypothesis for the next pass. Alias for 'history review'.
novel_features.rationale: Entry-type-aware append-only writes that link back to original_log_id. Enables the recommendations engine to learn from past decisions.

novel_features.name: Offline SQL query
novel_features.description: Read-only SQL REPL over the local SQLite store. SELECT/PRAGMA/EXPLAIN/WITH only. Use json_extract() to pull fields from the blob-stored resources.
novel_features.rationale: No MCP can ship this — they proxy the Graph API. A local mirror + SQL unlocks analysis MCPs can't do.

novel_features.name: Delta sync via activities cursor
novel_features.description: Incremental local-store sync using the generator's resumable cursor system. Full resync via --full; targeted resync via --resources.
novel_features.rationale: Meta has no updated_since filter; the generator's per-resource cursor tracking fills the gap.

novel_features.name: Creative fatigue detection (stub)
novel_features.description: Planned: find ads where CPM and frequency rise while CTR falls across a rolling window. Ships as a structural stub in this iteration — see command Long help for the SQL one-liner that reproduces today.
novel_features.rationale: Requires multi-day insights stored across a rolling window. The generator's /sync captures the most recent date-preset window only; the time-series slice isn't yet wired.

novel_features.name: Budget pacing monitor (stub)
novel_features.description: Planned: hourly spend-rate vs daily_budget with ETA-to-cap per campaign. Ships as a structural stub in this iteration.
novel_features.rationale: Requires hourly insights with minute-level time_increment. Current /sync uses day-level granularity.
